name: Earning Tree
description_short: Earning Tree Federal Credit Union is a community-focused credit union established in 1958, serving over 2,000 members in Wyoming, Pennsylvania with $42.5M in assets.
description_long: Earning Tree Federal Credit Union was established in 1958 and is headquartered in Wyoming, Pennsylvania. As a not-for-profit financial institution, it has grown to serve over 2,000 loyal members while stewarding $42.5 million in assets. The credit union operates a single main branch location at 800 Wyoming Avenue with one ATM and maintains a dedicated staff of over 5 employees. Earning Tree offers a comprehensive range of consumer and business banking services. Members can access checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), auto loans for new and used vehicles, personal loans, mortgage options (including fixed-rate and balloon/hybrid mortgages), and IRA/KEOGH retirement accounts. The credit union provides online and mobile banking platforms for both iOS and Android devices, online bill pay, online loan payments, drive-thru teller services, and financial calculators including mortgage and auto loan calculators. They also serve multiple financial, insurance, real estate, and trade organizations with tailored financial solutions. Earning Tree distinguishes itself as a member-owned institution with no stockholders, meaning profits benefit members through competitive rates and low fees. The credit union specifically targets multiple organizational communities beyond individual consumers, offering specialized solutions for various trade and professional groups. They maintain transparent service status reporting through an online dashboard where members can report outages or service issues across online banking, mobile app, website, phone, and ATM services. As a small, single-branch credit union in a specific geographic area, Earning Tree is best suited for individuals and organizations already connected to their membership-eligible communities and those who prefer local relationship banking. The limitation of one physical branch and one ATM may present inconvenience for members seeking broader geographic access or frequent in-person banking needs. Potential members should verify their eligibility through the listed financial, insurance, real estate, or trade organizations before assuming they can join.
services: Checking accounts (Share Draft accounts with 500+ members)
Online and mobile banking (iOS and Android)
pros: Not-for-profit structure means competitive rates and low fees with profits returning to members
Comprehensive loan offerings including mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, and flexible mortgage options (15-year fixed, balloon/hybrid)
Mobile banking app available for both iOS and Android platforms with full online banking access
Serves organizational communities with tailored financial solutions beyond individual consumers
Online bill pay and loan payment options available 24/7 for member convenience
Established 65+ year track record (founded 1958) with stable $42.5M asset base
Financial calculators provided for mortgages, auto loans, and financial planning tools
cons: Single branch location limits physical accessibility for members outside Wyoming, PA area
Only one ATM available, creating potential inconvenience for cash access and withdrawals
Membership eligibility restricted to specific financial, insurance, real estate, and trade organizations—not open to general public
Currently zero Money Market accounts and zero Share Certificates (CDs) listed as available products
Small institution with 5 employees may limit availability of specialized financial services or expertise
best_for: Members of eligible financial, insurance, real estate, or trade organizations seeking member-owned banking
Wyoming, PA residents or those with regular access to the Wyoming branch location
Consumers prioritizing local community banking relationships over branch network convenience
Borrowers seeking mortgage or auto loan products with competitive rates from a not-for-profit institution
diagnosis: Earning Tree Federal Credit Union is best for members of eligible organizational communities in or near Wyoming, PA who value member-owned banking and competitive rates. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted to specific professional and organizational groups, and the single-branch, limited-ATM infrastructure makes it impractical for members seeking broader geographic access or frequent in-person services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
